medical_history.history_of_present_illness: He started developing skin lesions a year prior to his colectomy, when he was hospitalized for UC. The skin lesions had resolved while his UC was being treated with prednisone, but flared shortly after prednisone was discontinued. Initial examination showed erythematous crusted papules over his scalp, eyelids, eyes, chin and chest. He was treated with oral amoxicillin/clavulanic acid for suspected folliculitis with some improvement. He subsequently developed new vesicles and crusted papules. Histology showed subepidermal bullous dermatosis with neutrophils and eosinophils. Direct immunofluorescence (DIF) showed linear BMZ deposition of IgA and fibrinogen in addition to some minimal epithelial cell surface IgA. A diagnosis of LABD was made.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Known UC, status postcolectomy with a residual rectal pouch and ileostomy

physical_examination.general_examination: Initial examination showed erythematous crusted papules over his scalp, eyelids, eyes, chin and chest.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Histology showed subepidermal bullous dermatosis with neutrophils and eosinophils. Direct immunofluorescence (DIF) showed linear BMZ deposition of IgA and fibrinogen in addition to some minimal epithelial cell surface IgA.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Linear BMZ deposition of IgA and fibrinogen in addition to some minimal epithelial cell surface IgA
Subepidermal bullous dermatosis with neutrophils and eosinophils
Cell surface IgA deposition and linear deposition of IgA along the BMZ
IgA cell surface antibody at a titre of 1 : 20
Intense grains of IgA on the basal keratinocyte cell surface

radiographic_examinations.findings: Pancolitis with ulcerations, consistent with UC
Diffuse colitis involving entire colon. Multiple biopsies showed diffuse chronic inflammation with cryptitis and crypt abscess formation, consistent with UC
Diffuse moderate inflammation with congestion and friability throughout the entire colon and distal proctitis, consistent with UC

other_tests.findings: Intraepithelial vesicles with acantholysis at all levels of the epithelium and a dense infiltrate of neutrophils with some eosinophils

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with erythematous crusted papules over his scalp, eyelids, eyes, chin, and chest. Initial treatment for suspected folliculitis with oral amoxicillin/clavulanic acid showed some improvement, but new vesicles and crusted papules developed. Histology showed subepidermal bullous dermatosis with neutrophils and eosinophils. Direct immunofluorescence (DIF) showed linear BMZ deposition of IgA and fibrinogen, leading to the diagnosis of LABD. The resolution of skin disease after rectal pouch removal supported the diagnosis.
